title: I&PR DIRECTOR IN SIAHA TLAWHIN DISTRICT OFFICE HMUNHMA TE A ENFIAH
content:
475/2026-2027

Siaha the 11th May 2026 :I& PR Director Pi K. Vanlalsangi  chuan vawiin khan District Information & Public Relations Office, Siaha a tlawh a. Pi K Ngosai, DIPRO Siaha leh a  Staff- te bakah Mizoram Journalist Association (MJA) Siaha ten lo dawngsawngin inkawmhona hun an hmang nghal a ni.

Director chuan lo dawngsawngtu Siaha I&PR a thawkte leh MJA Siaha te chungah lawmthu sawiin tha taka an thawhho thinna ah lawmthu a sawi a, hun lo la kal zel turah pawh thu dik leh rintlak thehdarh kawngah theihtawp chhuah tlang turin a fuih a ni.

MJA Siaha President Pu CZ Hluna pawhin thu sawiin Director Pi K Vanlalsangi chu lawmthu an hrilh a, I&PR Department te nen thawhhona tha tak an neih thu sawiin hma lam hunah pawh thahnem ngai taka an thawhdun zel tur thu a sawi a ni.

Pi K Vanlalsangi chuan Siaha District Office hmunhma leh ram te tlawh nghalin fur ruahtui tlak hmaa hmun thawm that ngai te a enfel pui a, an mamawh hrang hrangte a sawipui nghal a ni.

Attachment

title: STATE LEVEL DISHA COMMITTEE (DEVELOPMENT COORDINATION & MONITORING COMMITTEE) MEETING HELD UNDER THE CHAIRMANSHIP OF THE CHIEF MINISTER
content:
69/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, 11th May, 2026:The State Level DISHA Committee (DEVELOPMENT COORDINATION & MONITORING COMMITTEE) Meeting was held today at the Planning & Programme Implementation Conference Hall under the chairmanship of the Chief Minister, Shri Lalduhoma.

Among the key points highlighted by the Chief Minister were the following:

1.After the Government of India introduced the new financial management system, SNA-SPARSH, for the implementation and utilisation of Central Scheme funds, Mizoram has faced difficulties as some departments were unable to bring their accounts to zero balance by the end of the financial year. He stated that such situations should not recur and urged all concerned to remain vigilant.

2.He noted that departments implementing Central Schemes have been working in close coordination and that convergence among departments, wherever feasible, has strengthened implementation and improved outcomes. He encouraged departments to further intensify such collaborative efforts.

3.He appreciated the Irrigation & Water Resources Department for successfully securing the release of long-pending Central funds which had remained unreleased for many years.

4.He stressed that proper follow-up of proposals submitted under Central Schemes is essential and should not be left unattended. He said there are instances where intervention through the Chief Minister’s DO Letter becomes necessary and urged departments to inform him promptly whenever required so that follow-up action can be taken without delay. He also remarked that MP Shri Richard Vanlalhmangaiha has been highly supportive and approachable in such matters.

5.He expressed appreciation to all departments and reminded them that all developmental efforts are ultimately for the welfare of the State and its people, urging officials to continue giving their best.

During the meeting, it was informed that under the initiative of the Chief Minister, the proposed Mat Multipurpose Dam Project has now been prepared at an estimated cost of Rs. 274 crores. As per the proposal, the dam will have a height of 29 metres and will be capable of irrigating 700 hectares of agricultural land. The project is also designed to include power generation and eco-tourism components. The Brahmaputra Board will pursue funding support for the project.

Various departments implementing Central Schemes also presented reports on their achievements and activities for FY 2025–26. According to the report, under MGNREGA, a total of 62,193 households completed 100 days of employment. As the Government of India is set to rename the scheme as VB GRAMG, a corresponding Mizoram Scheme for its implementation has already been prepared and submitted for consideration by the Cabinet.

Regarding PMAY-G 2.0, the survey in Mizoram has been completed, and 54,601 households have been identified as eligible beneficiaries. It was observed that the current pattern of Central fund release under PMAY-G does not adequately align with Mizoram’s local conditions, and it was suggested that revisions to the guidelines be sought.

Under the SAGY (Model Village) initiative, all activities have been completed, and a closure report has already been submitted to the Central Government. It was also informed that no new SAGY scheme is expected to be introduced.

Under the Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Department, Animal Insurance under the National Livestock Mission is currently being implemented. Around 700 persons have availed the insurance coverage so far, which constitutes only around 10% of the expected target. The meeting suggested that awareness activities should be intensified.

According to the National Health Mission report, a total of 20,137 pregnant women were registered during the previous government year, of which 17,201 institutional deliveries were recorded. A total of 285 infant deaths were reported, while the Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) stands at 15. Since the target is to keep the IMR below 16, the target has been achieved.

The Planning & Programme Implementation Department report stated that Mizoram currently has 82 major development projects under the Ministry of Tribal Affairs. These include 19 projects under NESIDS (OTRI), 11 projects under NESIDS (Road), and 48 projects under NEC, besides several other proposals currently under submission.

The meeting also carried out a detailed review and discussion of the annual progress and performance of all departments implementing Central Schemes.

In today’s State Level DISHA Committee, Rural Development Minister Prof. Lalnilawma serves as Co-Chairman, while the Secretary of the Rural Development Department serves as Member Secretary. Members present included MP (Lok Sabha) Shri Richard Vanlalhmangaiha, MP (Rajya Sabha) Shri K. Vanlalvena, Shri T. Lalhlimpuia, MLA, Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga, Smt Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA, Shri K. Laldawngliana, MLA, and Shri K. Hrahmo, MLA, along with heads of around 30 departments implementing Central Schemes, representatives of NGOs, and other nominated members.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER HO IN STATE LEVEL DISHA COMMITTEE (DEVELOPMENT COORDINATION & MONITORING COMMITTEE) MEETING NEIH A NI
content:
474/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 11th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Planning & Programme Implementation Conference Hall-ah State Level DISHA Committee (SLDC) Meeting chu an Chairman, Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma kaihhruainain an thukhawm.

Chief Minister thusawi thenkhatte:

1. Sorkar laipuiin central scheme sum hman dan leh inpek dan kalphung thar SNA-SPARSH a hman tak hnuah Financial Year tawpa Account ti zero thei lo department kan awm avangin harsatna kan tawk a. Khatiang kha awm tawh lo se kan duh a. Kan zain i tang tlang ang u.

2. Central Scheme kalpuitu department-ten thawhho kan thiam a, a remchanna apianga kan inthlunzawm (convergence) hian hmalakna a chak a, kan hlawhpui zawk thin. I uar sauh sauh ang u.

3. Irrigation & Water Resources Department-in kum rei tak chhunga central sorkar rawn release theih loh kan fund te tun hnaiah an hnuk chhuak vek mai a. An chungah lawmthu ka sawi a ni.

4. Central Scheme kan kalpuinaah kan lehkha thehluhte kan umzui chak a ngai a. Sakuh ser nghah zui mai chi a ni lo. Chief Minister DO Letter han thawn a tul changte a awm bawk a. Inthlahrung lovin min rawn hriattir thuai ula, ka lo bawhzui zel ang. Kan MP Pu Richard Vanlalhmangaiha pawh a inhawng em em a ni tih kan hre dawn nia.

5. Department hrang hrangte chungah lawmthu ka sawi a. Kan hmalaknate hi kan ram leh hnam tan a ni tih hre rengin theihtawp i chhuah zel ang u, tih te an ni.

He meeting-ah hian Chief Minister hmalaknaa Mat Multipurpose Dam buaipui mek chu Rs. 274 Crore senna tur project duan fel a nih thu tarlan a ni a. Ruahman danah chuan tuikhuah hi meter 29 a sang, hectare 700 a zau leilet a chawm thei, Power siamna awm, Eco-Tourism kalpui theihna tura duan a ni a. Brahmaputra Board ten a thawhna tur sum an dap zui dawn a ni.

Meeting-ah hian Central Scheme thawk department hrang hrang ten FY 2025-26 a an hmalakna leh an hlawhtlinnate thlirho a ni a. Report a tarlan a nih danin, MGNREGA hnuaiah (household) 62,193 ten ni 100 inhlawhna an thawk a. Central sorkar chuan he scheme hi VB GRAMG tia a thlak dawn avangin a kalpuina tur Mizoram Scheme hi duan fel tawh a ni a. Cabinet Meeting-in a ngaihtuah tura thehluh a ni.

PMAY G 2.0 kalpuina turin Mizoramah survey neih zawh tawh a ni a. Chhungkaw 54,601 te chu he tanpuina dawng thei tura ngaih an ni. PMAY G sum central-in a pek chhuah dan hi Mizoram kalphung nen a inmil tawk lo nia ngaih a ni a. A guideline siam tha tura dil ni se, an ti.

SAGY (Model Village) hmalakna hi engkim thawh zawh tawh a ni a. Khar (Close) tura central sorkarah pawh lehkha thawn tawh niin, SAGY Scheme thar hi a awm tawh dawn lo nia hriat a ni.

AH & Vety hnuaiah Animal Insurance under National Livestock Missions kalpui mek a ni a. Mi 700 velin insurance hi an ti tawh a. Chu chu ti tura beisei zat atanga zaa sawm (10%) vel chauh an ni a. Uar zawka awareness kalpui ni se, an ti.

National Health Missions report atanga a lan danin sorkar kum kalta chhunga nu naupai inziaklut zawng zawng hi 20,137 an ni a. Damdawiina nau nei (Institutional Delivery) hi 17,201 an ni. Nau piang hlim boral hi 285 an awm a, Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) hi 15 a ni a. 16 aia hniama awm hi target mek a ni a, target hi hlen (achieve) a ni.

Planning & Programme Implementation Report-a a lan danin Mizoramah Ministry of Tribal Affairs hnuaia hmasawnna hnathawh pawimawh tak tak 82 a awm mek a, NESIDS (OTRI) hnuaiah project 19, NESIDS (Road) hnuaiah project 11 leh NEC hnuaiah project 48 te kalpui mek an ni. Hengte bakah hian ruahmanna thehluh mek eng emaw zat an awm bawk.

Meeting-ah hian central scheme kalpui department zawng zawngte kum khat chhung hmalakna chipchiar taka endik leh sawiho an ni.

State Level DISHA Committee-ah hian Rural Development Minister Prof. Lalnilawma chu Co-Chairman niin, RD Department Secretary chu Member Secretary a ni a. Member ah MP (Lok Sabha) Pu Richard Vanlalhmangaiha, MP (Rajya Sabha) Pu K. Vanlalvena te bakah, Pu T. Lalhlimpuia, MLA, Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga, Pi Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA, Pu K. Laldawngliana, MLA  leh Pu K. Hrahmo, MLA te, Central Scheme kalpuitu department 30 bawr hotute leh mimal leh tlawmngai pawl aiawh ruat bikte an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: KMMTP and Railway Impacts Study Committee thukhawm
content:
473/2026-2027

Aizawl, 11th May, 2026 :Vawiin khan KMMTTP and Railway Impacts Study Committee chu Chairman Pu T. Lalhlimpuia MLA hovin Secretariat Conference Hall, MINECO-ah an thukhawm.

He hunah hian Chairman Pu T. Lalhlimpuia chuan Study Committee in nikum lam atanga nasa taka hma an lak tawh thu te, thil tam tak hmuhchhuah an neih thu te, Sawrkar leh mipui in buatsaih a tul thu te a sawi a. Pu Zomuana Member Study committee in presentation neiin study commiitee hma lak tawhna entirin an  thlir ho a, hma chhawp awm mek te uluk takin an thlir a, activity kalpui zel dan tur ruahmana thar  an siam bawk. Committee member dept hrang hrang aiawh te pawhin, an dept ten he Kaladan Multi Model Transport Transits Project (MKKTTP) leh Railway in Mizo mipui te tana remchang a siam tura mipui te leh thalai te buatsaih dan tura rawtna an neih te an sawi a, mumal zawkin meeting leh huna thlir ho tura ziak a committee a thlen tura tih an ni. Awareness programme a pawimawh rualin, heng project lian tak tak ten remchanna a thlen turah hian, infrastructure pawimawh din ngai leh tul te, en tel nise an ti bawk. Meeting ah  hian Pu Malsawmzual Ralte Mizoram Youth Commission pawh telin, MYC in tih theih tam tak,a bikin thalai te, an neih thu te, a hmun ngeia Youth Commission pawhin programme remchang siam an tum thu a sawi bawk.

Attachment

title: Art & Culture Minister-in Ephatha Special School kum 10 tlin lawmna a hmanpui
content:
472/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 11th May 2026:Vawiin khan Ephatha Special School for the Deaf & Hard of Hearing chuan R.S.T.C Building, College Veng-ah an school kum 10 tlin lawmna hun an hmang a. He hun hi Art & Culture Department changtu Minister Pu C. Lalsawivunga chuan khuallian niin a hmanpui.

He hunah hian Art& Culture Minister, bialtu MLA ni bawk Pu C. Lalsawivunga chuan thu sawiin Ephatha Special School-in kum 10 an tling thei chu a lawmpui thu a sawi a. Zirlaite chu an nungchang mawi tak leh zirtirtute thu an awihna chu chhawm nung zel turin a chah a. Ephatha Society of Mizoram for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (ESOMDHH) te inpekna leh nu leh pa te nena an tanrualna chu ropui a tih thu leh Principal leh zirtirtuten naupangte an enkawl dan chu fakawm a tih thu a sawi a. Zirlai naupangte chu fuihin vohbik an nihna chu mahni indahhniam phahnaa hmang mai lo tur leh eizawnna kawng an neih theihna tura lehkha tha taka zir zel turin a chah a, sawrkar pawhin a theihna apiangah a ngaihsak em em a ni tih hre reng turin a chah bawk a ni.

Ephatha Special School for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing hi Ephatha Society of Mizoram for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (ESOMDHH) enkawl niin, tun dinhmunah hian zirlai 32 awmin Indian Sign Language hmangin zirtirna pek thin an ni a. Vawiin programme-a tarlan a nih danin Ephatha Special School chuan India rama a hmasa ber atan Mizoram State Commission for Protection of Child Rights nen thawk hovin POCSO Act chu Indian Sign Language hmangin video an siam a. Ephatha Special School hian Mizoram State Council for Child Welfare buatsaih National Painting Competition(State Level)-ah pawh kum 2022, 2024 leh 2025-ah te khan pakhatna dinhmun Junior leh Senior category-ah an lo hauh tawh a. Bengngawng tawngtheilo ten Pathian thu an ngaihthlak ve theihna turin Sunday Service for the Deaf chu ESOMDHH hmalaknain kum 2024 atang khan Ramhlun East Presbyterian biakinah hun hman thin a ni bawk.

Vawiina hun hman hi ESOMDHH General Secretary Pi Jenny Lalbiaktlingi chuan kaihhruaiin, ESOMDHH President Pi Saihmingthangi'n kalkhawmte lawmna thu a sawi a. Hemi zawh hian Ephatha Special School Principal Pu Samuel R.Lalhmangaiha chuan report pein Senior Advisor Pu Lalbuatsaiha hnen atangin thusawi ngaihthlak a ni a. Vohbik naupangten special item mit leh beng titlai tak tak an entir bawk a. Pi Jenny Lalbiaktlingi chuan lawmthu sawiin hun hi a khar a ni.

Attachment

title: AIZAWL DC THUCHHUAH
content:
471/2026-2027

Aizawl the 11th May, 2026:African Swine Fever (ASF) darh zel tur dan nan leh mipuite kan lo him a, kan lo fimkhur zel nan Aizawl DC chuan Section 163 of BNSS, 2023 in thuneihna a pek angin, Aizawl District huam chhunga mipuite bakah vawk vulhtute leh vawk hmanga eizawngte zawm turin a hnuaia thupek hi a tichhuak.

He thupek hi tih danglam a nih loh chuan chhuah a nih atangin thia hnih (2 months) chhung a huam anga. Dan bawhchhia te chu Section 223 of BNS 2023 hmangin hrem an ni ang.

title: MPSC Hriattirna
content:
470/2026-2027

Aizawl the 11thMay, 2026 :Mizoram Public Service Commission Online Portal chu khawl lama harsatna a awm avangin One Time Password (OTP) chu SMS/message kaltlanga dawn theih a ni lova, candidate-te’n harsatna an tawh avangin pawi an tih thu May ni 8, 2026 khan thuchhuah an siam.

Hemi a nih avang hian Commission-in NotificationNo.A.334012/5/2025- MPSC(PRE) dt 8thApril, 2026hmanga a lo chhuah tawh Post hrang hrang tana Departmental Examination buatsaih turah applicant-te in-register hman lo an awm a. Chuvangin Departmental Exam la ‘clear’ lote tan dilna thehluh (submit) hun chhung chu ni 18/05/2026 (Thawhtanni), tlai dar 4:00 PM thleng pawhsei a ni.

title: Mizoram Police ten Year of Discipline hmang tan
content:
469/2026-2027

Aizawl the 11th May, 2026:Mizoram Police chuan kum 2026–2027 chu “Year of Discipline” atan May 6, 2026 khan Home Minister Pu K Sapdanga hoa programme buatsaihah an puang a. Vawiin khan Mizoram chhunga an unit hrang hrang ten anmahni hmun theuhah programme urhsun tak buatsaihin a takin an hmang tan ta a ni.

Year of Discipline” hi Mizoram Police Fitness Improvement Programme hnuaia kalpui mek, “Train to Excel” tihchangtlunna leh chhunzawmna a ni a. Mizoram Police Fitness Improvement Programme hi kum khat aia rei kalpui a lo ni tawh a ni. Mizoram Police mi leh sa ten taksa, rilru leh thlarau lam thlenga hriselna tha an neih theihna tur leh tha leh zuala hna an hlen chhuah theihna tur a nih beiseiin he ruahmanna hi Home Minister kaihhruainaa duan chhuah a ni.

He kumpuan, “Year of Discipline” hi Mizoram Police awmna hmun hrang hrangah urhsun taka kalpui tur niin, a lal ber atanga a rank hniam ber thlengin tha taka zawm tur a ni a. Mizoram Police chuan ram chhunga dan leh hrai kenkawh kawngah rintlak leh hlawhtling zawka rawngbawl an tum a ni.

Attachment

title: Mizoramah E Stamping hman a nih theih nan inremna ziak
content:
468/2026-2027

Aizawl the 11th May, 2026:Ram lei leh hralh bakah inremna hrang hrang siam nana hman thin Non Judicial stamp paper aiah e-stamping hman a nih theih nan vawiin chawhma dar 11 khan Land Revenue & Settlement (LR&S) Minister pisaah LR&S Department leh Stock Holding Corporation of India ten thawh hona thuthuthlung an ziak. He thawhhona thuthlungah hian Mizoram Sawrkar aiawhin LR&S Secretary Pu Vanlalmawia, IAS, leh SHCIL aiawhin Regional Manager (East & North East) Pu Pritesh Kumar ten hming an ziak a ni.

Thu sawiin Minister Pu.B.Lalchhanzova chuan ram emaw, in leh lo emaw, lei leh hralh bakah mimal leh pawl inkara inremna siam nemngheh nan Non judicial stamp hman thin niin, chu chu sawrkarah register thin a ni a. Inremna a zira stamp duty chawi a ngaih thin thu leh, chu Stamp duty chu lehkha (paper)-a lei thin a ni, a ti a. Stamp paper awm sa a zawh chang te, denomination mamawh ang a awm loh chang te a awm thin bakah stamp paper lem (fake) siam an lo awm thin tih sawiin Revenue Minister chuan heng harsatna te hi nuaibo a nih theih nan June ni 2, 2024-khan sawrkar chuan The Mizoram Stamp Rules siam tha (amend)-in electronic hmanga stamp duty pek phal a ni, tih a sawi a; chumi bawhzuina atan thawhhona thuthlung ziah a lo ni ta hi a lawmawm hle a ni, a ti.

Minister chuan Stamp duty chu State Sawrkar chan liau liau a nih angin a lakkhawm kawngah thahnem ngaih a tul thu sawiin kum 2024/25 a Stamp Duty tlingkhawm chu vbc 13.45 a ni a, kum 2025/26 ah chuan vbc 20.2 ah a pung a ni, a ti a. Kum 2026/27 chhungin Finance Department chuan stamp duty atangin cheng vbc 22.5 a bituk a, e-stamping hman a nih dawn tak piah lamah Sub Division level a Sub Registration Office hawn a nih tak avangin Finance Department bituk zat hi khum ngei an inbeisei thu a sawi a ni.

Pu B. Lalchhanzova chuan stamp duty lak chungchangah e-stamping hmang tura department-in hma an lakna kawngah harsatna hrang hrang an tawh thu sawiin tuna a hlawhtlinna an hmu chho chu LR&S Department-a a thawhpui te thawhrimna rah a nih thu a sawi a ni.

E-stamping hi a bul tan nan Aizawl District ah Pilot project atan kalpui a ni dawn a. A tahtawlin District dangah pawh kalpui vek a ni ang. Pilot project hi ni 45 chhunga tihfel tura duan a ni a. A hlawhtlin hunah chuan mahni awm hmunah emaw, Accredited collection centres atangin emaw, stamp duty chu pek theih a ni tawh dawn a. Hei bakah hian stamp indaihlo leh chatlak a awm tawh dawn lo a. Stamp duty hralhna leh leina bo thlau pawh a nuai bo dawn a ni tih Pu Pritesh Kumar, Regional Manager, SHCIL chuan a sawi.

E-stamping chungchanga thawhona thuthlung ziahnaah hian Pu R.Lalthazuala, MCS, Director LR&S chuan a hman dan tur te, mipui leh sawrkar tanna awlsam a thlen dan tur te a sawifiah a ni.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER FLAGS OFF FIRST BATCH OF MIZO TERRITORIAL ARMY
content:
68/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 11th May, 2026:Chief Minister, Shri Lalduhoma, today flagged off the first batch of 144 Mizo youths selected for the 166 Infantry Battalion Territorial Army (Home & Hearth), Assam at the Assembly Annexe Building Conference Hall. The batch, comprising 135 males and 9 females, is scheduled to leave for Shillong on the 15th of this month for their training.

The event was attended by Home Minister Shri K. Sapdanga; Lt. Col. (Retd) Clement Lalhmingthanga, MLA (Champhai South) and Chairman of the Territorial Army (TA) Establishment & Coordination Committee; Home Secretary Shri Vanlalmawia, IAS; Brig. Manoj Kumar Patel, SC, Commander, TA Group Eastern Command; along with other distinguished officers from the Armed Forces and Assam Rifles, and senior state officials.

Speaking on the occasion, the Chief Minister said that today is a proud and historic day for Mizoram. He stated that previously, he himself was not aware of the Territorial Army, but after learning about it, he immediately appealed to the Central Government authorities to sanction a Mizo Battalion. Initially, permission was granted to recruit 70 personnel, but further negotiations resulted in the approval of 144 personnel before recruitment even began. He expressed gratitude to Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modiji, Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shahji , Defence Minister Shri Rajnath Singhji and Chief of Army Staff General Upendra Dwivedi for this.

The Chief Minister further added that efforts are already underway to secure additional recruitment quotas. He encouraged as many Mizo youths as possible to join the Territorial Army, stating that besides being a source of employment, the TA is a noble service dedicated to national security and also serves as a path toward disciplined and responsible living.

Addressing the newly recruited youths, the Chief Minister said that the 144 recruits being sent for training today would serve as pioneers and representatives of Mizo youth. He urged them to give their best, to uphold and showcase the good values and character of the Mizo people wherever they go, and strive to bring honour to the state and its people.

Home Minister Shri K. Sapdanga also addressed the gathering and said that the Territorial Army combines Civilian Excellence with Military Discipline, bringing honour, dignity, and character-building opportunities. Congratulating the new recruits, he urged them to always give their best, and faithfully carry out their responsibilities and duties. He also reminded them whenever they feel discouraged during their training, they should always remember that “Difficult roads often lead to beautiful destinations."

Lt. Col. (Retd) Clement Lalhmingthanga, in his speech, stated that the Mizo Territorial Army became a reality through the initiative and effort of Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma and described it as a historic achievement that will always be remembered in Mizoram's history. He further added that efforts for further recruitment are ongoing, and they are hopeful that a new advertisement may be released around September this year.

Brig. Manoj Kumar Patel also credited the Chief Minister's initiative and hardwork for the establishment of the Mizo Territorial Army and expressed his gratitude to the Chief Minister for it. He further noted that there are still many vacancies within the Territorial Army that they desire to fill.

The MTA recruits will begin their training from next month. Prior to that, they will depart for Shillong later this month to complete the necessary formalities. While the 135 male recruits will undergo their training in Shillong, the 9 female recruits will proceed to Bangalore for their training.

Attachment

title: SP MAMIT THUPEK CHHUAH
content:
467/2026-2027

Dated: Mamit the 11th of May, 2026 :PWD, National Highway Division - I ten NH 108 atanga Tuidam inkar kawng (Kawng hlui) chu siamthat hna an thawh dawn avangin Lirthei kal dan tur ruahmanna siam a tul a.

Chuvangin, Pu Manny Bhushan Singh, IPS, Superintendent of Police, Mamit District chuan Motor Vehicle Act, 1988 (Act 59 of 1988) section 115 leh Mizoram Sawrkar Notification No. B.12021/10/2004-TRP Dt. 01.06.2020 in thuneihna a pek angin NH 108 atanga Tuidam inkar kawng (Kawng hlui) a Lirthei kal dan tur hetiang hian thupek a chhuah.

I. LMV chin chunglam tan:

Monday-Saturday - Road Block 9:00 AM–1:00 PM - Road Open 1:00 PM–2:00 PM - Road Block 2:00 PM–5:00 PM - Road Open 5:00 PM–9:00 AM

Sunday - Road Block 08: 00 AM -1:00 PM - Road Open 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - Road Block 2:00 PM - 6:00 PM - Road Open 5:00 PM- 8:00 AM

HMV chin leh a chunglam tan:

Monday - Load la tur Tuidam lam panTuesday - Balu/Load phur - Mamit lam panWednesday - Load la tur, Tuidam lam panThursday - Balu/Load phurFriday - Load la tur Tuidam lam panSaturday - Balu/Load phurSunday - Load la tur-Tuidam lam pan.

Kawng tha zawk kan neihna tur a nih avangin thupek ngaipawimawha zawm theuh turin kan in ngenin kan inhriattir a. Thupek zawmlo te chu 179 MV Act hmanga hrem an ni ang.

title: CHIEF MINISTER-IN MIZO TERRITORIAL ARMY BATCH HMASA BER A VAILIAM
content:
456/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 11th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan Assembly Annexe Building Conference Hall-ah 166 Infantry Battalion Territorial Army (Home & Hearth) a thawk tur, Mizo thalai thlan chhuah hmasa ber mi 144, mipa 135 leh hmeichhia 9-te chu a vailiam a. Tun thla ni 15 khian training turin Shillong an pan ang.

He hunah hian Home Minister Pu K. Sapdanga; Champhai South MLA leh Territorial Army (TA) Establishment & Coordination Committee Chairman ni bawk Lt. Col. (Retd) Clement Lalhmingthanga; Home Secretary Pu Vanlalmawia; TA Group Eastern Command Commander- Shaurya Chakra, Brig.Manoj Kumar Patel te bakah Armed Forces leh Assam Rifles officer dangte leh state official pawimawh dangte an tel a ni.

Thusawiin Chief Minister chuan, vawiin hi Mizoram tana ni lawmawm leh chhinchhiahtlak a ni. Territorial Army hi a awm tih pawh tunhmain ka lo la hre ngai lo va, kan hriat rualin central sorkar thuneitute hnenah Mizo Battalion min pe ve turin ka dil nghal a. A tirah mi 70 lak min phalsak a, a taka kan lak hmain kan dil belh leh a, mi 144 lak min phalsak ta a. Prime Minister Pu Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Pu Amit Shah, Defence Minister Pu Rajnath Singh leh Chief of Army Staff General Upendra Dwivedi te chungah kan lawm hle.

A dang lak belhna tur hmalakna kan buaipui leh mek bawk a. Mizo thalai, a tam thei ang ber hi lut se, eizawnna a nih piah lamah ram venhimna hna a ni a, a zahawm a, nunkawng kaihruaitu tha a ni bawk si a. Thahnem kan ngai hle a ni.

Vawiina training tura kan vailiam tak kan thalai mi 144 te hi hmahruaitu, Mizo thalaite hmelhmang tilangtu tur in nih avangin theihtawp chhuah turin kan beisei che u a. In kalna apiangah zo nun ze mawi tak takte lantir zelin, kan ram leh hnam timawitu nih in tum tlat dawn nia, tiin a chah.

Home Minister Pu K. Sapdanga pawhin thu sawiin, Territorial Army hian thiamna (Civilian Excellence) leh sipai discipline (Military discipline) tha a keng dun a, zahawmna leh hmingthanna a thlen theih bakah nungchang siamthatnate a keng a. A tha em em a ni, a ti a. He hnaa tling tharte lawmpuiin theihtawp chhuah zui tur leh an mawhphurhnate hlen ngei turin a chah a. Training laia an lo hnual deuh a nih pawhin, 'Kawng bumboh leh harsa hian hmun mawi takah min hruai thin a ni,' tih hre reng turin a chah a ni.

Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga, pawhin thusawiin Mizo Territorial Army chu Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma hmalakna vanga lo awm a nih thu a sawi a. Mizoram tana hriatreng tawh tur hmasawnna chhinchhiahtlak a ni, a ti a. Lak belh kan dil leh mek a, kumin September thlaa Advertisement tihchhuah hman kan beisei dawn a ni, a ti.

Brig. Manoj Kumar Patel pawhin thusawiin Mizo Territorial Army hi Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma hmalakna leh a thawhrimna zara lo awm a ni a, a chungah kan lawm a ni, a ti a. Territorial Army post ruak a la tam thu sawiin, a theih chuan hnawhkhat zel kan duh a ni, a ti.

Thla thar atang khian MTA-te chuan training an nei tan dawn a. Chu mi hmaa tihfel ngaite tifel turin tun thla laihawl hian Shillong an pan phawt ang a. Mipa 135 te hian Shillong-ah Training an nei zui ang a, hmeichhia 9 te hian Bangalore-ah training an kal thung ang.

Attachment

title: Health Minister Pi Lalrinpuii'n Lunglei Electric Venga sa talhna in (slaughter house) a tlawh
content:
455/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 11:Tukin zingkar khan Health & Family Welfare Minister Pi Lalrinpuii (Lunglei East bialtu MLA ni bawk) chuan a bial chhung, Lunglei Electric Venga awm, Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Department hnuaia Slaughter House (Sa Talhna In) chu a hmunah tlawhin, ṭangkai taka hman theih dan tur a enfiah.

Minister hi Lunglei Municipal Council (LMC) Chairman Pu Lalzuithanga, Vice Chairman Pu K. Lalrinawma, Ward VI Councillor Pu R. Lalremsiama, khawtlang hruaitute leh sa talhtu pawl ai awhte'n ṭawiawmin, AH & Vety Department Joint Director Dr Ngurzidingi Sailo, District AH & Vety Officer Dr Lalengzami Hrahsel, Integrated AH & Vety Training Institute Principal Dr Lalmuanpuia Samte leh thawktu dangte'n a hmunah an lo dawngsawng a. Slaughter House hmunhma fang hovin, a dinhmun an en ho bakah, ṭangkai taka hman zui a nih theih dan tur ruahmanna hrang hrang an sawi ho.

Slaughter House hi kum 2013 khan AH & Vety Department-in a sak bul ṭanin, kum 2015-ah hman theiha sak zawh fel a ni a. Chhan hrang hrang avangin hman ṭangkai lohvin a awm zui a. Bialtu MLA & Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan hei hi ngai pawimawhin, a vei hle a, a hmunah tlawhin ṭangkai taka hman a nih theih nana hma la turin LMC hotute, AH & Vety Department leh sa talhtute chu khunkhan takin a sawipui a ni.

Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan ran talh avangin natna hrik inkai theih tam tak a awm a, khaw chhunga sa talh mai mai chu a him lovin, a hrisel lo tih sawiin, Lunglei khawpuia mihring pung chho zel avang leh LMC a lo din tak avangin inkaihhruaina fel tak nen sa talhna in chu hman ṭangkai dan ngaihtuah a ṭul tih a sawi a. Sa talhna in chu Lunglei mipuite'n sa thianghlim leh hrisel an ei theihna atana ṭangkai tak tur a nih thu sawiin, sawrkar thuneitute leh department lam nen ṭang hovin a hma thei ang bera hman theih turin hma lak an tum thu a sawi.

Minister hma lakna avang hian Power & Electricity Department lam pawhin Slaughter House bulah hian transformer thar bun turin hma an lak nghal tur thu an sawi bawk.

Attachment

title: INFORMATION & PUBLIC RELATIONS DEPARTMENT DIRECTOR IN LAWNGTLAI TLAWH
content:
454/2026-2027

Lawngtlai,11th May 2026 :Vawiin khan I&PR Director Pi K. Vanlalsangi chuan District Information & Public Relations Office, Lawngtlai a tlawh a. Pu Zonunchama Ralte, DIPRO, Lawngtlai, leh Staff- te bakah Mizoram Journalist Association (MJA), Lawngtlai, ten lo dawngsawngin inkawmhona hun an hmang nghal a ni.

Pi K. Vanlalsangi chuan DIPRO, Lawngtlai leh MJA, Lawngtlai te inkar a thawhhona tha tak awm thin chu lawmawm a tih thu tarlangin, hmalam hunah pawh thawhhona tha zawk nei zel turin a sawm a. Sawrkar thuchhuak dik taka thehdarh bakah department - te hmalakna kawng hrang hrang puanzar kawngah DIPRO Office chuan mawhphurhna an nei lian hle tih tarlangin, sawrkar rin tlaka thawk thin turin a chah a. MJA leh I&PR- te chu ram leh mipuite tana thawkho, innghen tlat an nih angin, ram leh mipuite tana thawkho zel tura fuihin, thuthar thehdarh leh sawrkar hmalakna puanzar kawngah District - mipuite tan a theihtawp chhuah zel turin a sawm nghal bawk.

MJA, Lawngtlai chuan I&PR Director in Lawngtlai a lo tlawh thei chu lawmawm an tih thu tarlangin, heihian rah tha a chhuah ngei an beisei tih an sawi a. MJA leh I&PR chu kawng engkima thawkho tlat an nih thu tarlangin, sawrkar thuchhuak, hmalakna leh mipuite hriat tur pawimawh thehdarh kawngah theihtawp an chhuah tlan thin thu an tarlang a. Ram leh hnam siam that kawngah MJA leh I& PR chuan mawhphurhna lian tak an nei tih sawiin, hma lam hunah thahnem ngai taka an thawhdun zel tur thu an tarlang a ni.

Pi K. Vanalsangi hian DIPRO Office Lawngtlai hmunhma leh Buliding - te a enfiah nghal a. Vawiin vek hian DIPRO Office Siaha a tlawh nghal bawk a ni.

Attachment

title: KHAWZAWL KVK-IN SCIENTIST-FARMER INKAWMNA LEH THLAICHI PEK CHHUAHNA HUN HMANG
content:
453/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 11th May, 2026 :Vawiin khan KVK Khawzawl chuan Senior Scientist & Head Dr. Malsawmkimi kaihhruaiin KVK Training hall-ah Farmers-Scientist inkawmna hun an hmang a. He hunah hian Tualpui khuaa kuthnathawktu chungkaw 21-te kalkhawmin, thlaichin kawnga an harsatna hrang hrangte sawi khawmin hun hlawk tak an hmang a ni. Kalkhawmte chu thlaichi chi hrang hrang Indian Institute of Horticulture Research (IIHR-NEH) Scheme hnuai atangin pek nghal anni bawk.

title: African Swine Fever (ASF) chungchanga Siaha District Magistrate thuchhuah
content:
452/2026-2027

Siaha the 11th May 2026:District Magistrate Pu VL Hruaizela Khiangte chuan a hnuai tarlan ang hian Vawk hri (African Swine Fever) chungchanga Siaha mipuite hriattur thuchhuah a siam.

ORDER

District AH & Vety. Officer, Siaha District, Siaha hnen atanga ngenna kan hmuh angin hun engemaw chen Vawk natna hri hlauhawm tak African Swine Fever (ASF) in min kiansan hnuin khua a ro in a rawn lum chho leh hle tawh mai a, District thenkhat Aizawl leh Saitualah te Vawk thi report a awm leh tawh a, kan District ah pawh Vawk kan vulh chho leh tan mek a ni a, kan Vawk neih chhun chhun te, he hri hlauhawm tak atanga an lo him theih nan khuahkhirhna thupek 'ORDER' chhuah alo tul leh ta a.

Chuvangin, kei, VL HRUAIZELA KHIANGTE, DISTRICT MAGISTRATE, SIAHA DISTRICT, hian Section 163 of Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023-in thuneihna min pek angin, hetiang hian thupek ka chhuah e.

1. Siaha District huamchhungah, Ram dang, State dang leh District dang atangin Vawk, Vawksa leh Vawksa atanga eitur siam chi reng reng lakluh khap tlat a ni.

2. Siaha District huamchhung khua leh khua inkarah Vawk, Vawksa leh Vawksa atanga eitur siam chi reng reng inlakpawh tawn khap tlat a ni.

3. Vawk damlo reng reng talh khap tlat a ni a, Vawk damlo chu dah hran vat tur ni.

4. Vawk damlo nei te'n arang lamin AH & Vety Hospital, Govt. of Mizoram, Siaha ah emaw mahni Khua/Veng/VFA/VC/Task Force te hnenah hriattir vat tur a ni.

5. Vawk alo thi anih chuan mahni duhduhna ah paih mai lovin Disinfectants, Chinai leh Bleaching etc. nen thuk taka phum tur a ni.

Hemi dan bawhchhetute chu Section 223 of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 hmanga na taka hrem an ni ang.

He thupek hian chhuah a nih atanga thla hnih (2) chhung a huam ang.

He thupek hi nemnghetin vawiin ni 06th May, 2026 hian hming ka ziah hnan e.

Attachment

title: Mamit-ah Mother’s Day 2026 pualin hun hmang
content:
451/2026-2027

Dated: Mamit the 11th of May,2026:Vawiin khan Women & Child Development, Mamit District buatsaihin Nu te Ni (Mother’s Day) Pi Carolyn LB Khiangte, Addl DC hovin Mamit Anganwadi Centre-IX ah hman a ni.

Pi Carolyn LB Khiangte chuan Nute chu harsatna hrang hrang tawk thin mah ila Nu kan nihna hre renga kan fate dah pawimawh hmasa ber reng tlat turin a chah a. Kan fate kan dah pawimawh tlata theih tawp kan chhuah reng chuan nakinah kan tan lawmman tha tak a la awm dawn a ni tiin Nuho kalkhawmte chu a fuih.

He hun kaihruaitu Pu Lalruatmawia Ralte, District Programme Officer, Mamit chuan Mother's Day chu Nu ten an fate tana an inpekna te, chhungkua leh khawtlang tana an tha thawh te hriat sak leh ngaihhlut loha awm thin hriat reng sakna leh ngaihhlutna lantirna hun atan hman a nih thu a sawi.

He Mother's Day pual hian Nu entawntlak leh chhuanawm, a pasal a sun hnua amaha fate puitling leh entawntlak tak taka enkawl seiliantu Pi Zorampari, Mamit Bazar Veng chu chawimawi thilpek hlan a ni.

title: CHIEF MINISTER MEETS DoNER SECRETARY
content:
67/2026-2027

Aizawl, 11th May 2026:Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma and Pu Sanjay Jaju, Secretary, Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER), met at the Chief Minister’s official residence yesterday evening. The main points discussed during the meeting are as follows:

The Chief Minister expressed his gratitude to the Secretary for the approval of the NEC-funded project “Sustainable Cultivation of Mizo Ginger USP.” He stated that Mizoram produces high-quality and pure ginger, and that NITI Aayog has even recognized Mizoram as the “Ginger Capital of India.” He added that the approved fund would greatly benefit ginger cultivators across the state.

The Chief Minister also raised the need for a more systematic and transparent implementation of the 10% Gross Budgetary Support (GBS). The DoNER Secretary assured him that the matter would be looked into.

Under the NESIDS (Roads) scheme, the Khankawn to Rongura Hmun road can reportedly be extended by about 1.70 km to connect with the Thenzawl–Buarpui Intermediate Lane, which would be highly beneficial. The Chief Minister requested that the extension be considered, and the DoNER Secretary assured that necessary steps would be taken.

Pu Sanjay Jaju informed the Chief Minister that efforts are being made to facilitate the entry of private investors for the development and upgradation of the Thenzawl Golf Resort. They also discussed several pending DoNER scheme projects concerning the Government of Mizoram.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER LEH DONER SECRETARY INKAWM
content:
450/2026-2027

Aizawl the 11th May 2026:Nimin tlai khan Pu Lalduhoma, Chief Minister leh Pu Sanjay Jaju, Secretary, Ministry of DoNER te chu CM Bangla-ah an inkawm. An thusawi tlangpui te chu:

1. Chief Minister hian Sustainable Cultivation of Mizo Ginder USP NEC fund tur approved a nih avangin Secretary hnenah hian lawmthu a sawi a. Mizoramin sawhthing kan thar tha a, kan sawhthingte hi a quality a thain a thianghlim a. NITI Aayog chuan Ginger Capital of India-ah min puang hial a ni. He fund hi sawhthing chingtute tan a tangkai hle dawn a ni, a ti.

2. Chief Minister hian 10% GBS mumal zawk leh langtlang zawka kalpui a nih theih nana hmalak a tul thu a lo hrilh a. DoNER Secretary hian lo enzui a tiam a ni.

3. NESIDS (Roads) hnuaia Khankawn to Rongura Hmun hi 1.70 km vela sei laih belhin Thenzawl Buarpui Intermediate Lane a pawh thei a. A tangkai hle dawn a. Chuvang chuan a laih zawmna min ngaihtuahpui ula tia a rawtna pawh DoNER Secretary hian hma a lak tur thu a hrilh a ni.

4. Pu Sanjay Jaju hian Thenzawl Golf Resort tihchangtlunna atan private investor te an luh theihna tura hma lak a tum thu Chief Minister hi a hrilh. Mizoram sawrkar pending DoNER scheme project thenkhat te an sawi dun bawk.

Attachment
